summary: PR 2665 linked src/melee/it/itdrop.c by changing configure.py from NonMatching to Matching, adjusting itdrop.c stack padding and zero-float handling, and updating GALE01 .sdata2 symbol metadata around 0x804DC6B8. The bot report shows two new function matches in main/melee/it/itdrop, it_8026F3D4 and it_8026F6BC, while also reporting a broken .sdata2 match.

key_files.role: Primary item source file linked as matching; changed zero-float initialization, local stack padding, and assignments in it_8026F3D4, it_8026F53C, it_8026F5C8, it_8026F6BC, it_8026F7C8, and it_8026F8B4.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists main/melee/it/itdrop function matches

key_files.role: Build configuration changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/itdrop.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/itdrop.c").
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk near MatchingFor object list

key_files.role: Symbol metadata changed three .sdata2 entries at 0x804DC6B8, 0x804DC6BC, and 0x804DC6C0 from global it_804DC6B8/BC/C0 names to local zero_init, @217, and @218.

what_changed: Marked melee/it/itdrop.c as Matching in configure.py.
Added static const f32 zero_init[1] = { 0.0f } in itdrop.c with an explicit @todo saying it is a hack needed to match it_8026F3D4 while keeping this the only 0.0f in the file.
Replaced several direct 0.0f assignments in itdrop.c with zero_init[0], and in it_8026F3D4 loaded a local zero through a volatile f32 pointer before assigning sp24.x/y/z.
Adjusted manual stack padding in it_8026F3D4 from _padA[0x48] to _padA[0x4C] and _padC[8] to _padC[4].
Adjusted PAD_STACK in it_8026F6BC from 96 to 88; PAD_STACK(12) remained present in it_8026F5C8.
Updated .sdata2 symbol names/scopes near 0x804DC6B8, including introducing local symbol zero_init.
Bot report recorded +628 matched code bytes, two new function matches, and a .sdata2 regression from 100.00% to 85.71%.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the matching scope narrow: one item source file, its build configuration status, and nearby symbol metadata.
The author documented the unusual zero_init tactic in-source with a @todo rather than leaving it unexplained.
The bot report made the tradeoff visible: text-section function matches improved while .sdata2 matching regressed.

decomp_lessons.text: When linking a previously NonMatching translation unit, the PR may need coordinated edits in source, configure.py, and symbols metadata; keep those coupled edits easy to audit.
A code-match improvement can coexist with a data-section regression; the bot report should be preserved as evidence rather than treating the match as risk-free.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: +628 matched code bytes and .sdata2 broken match

decomp_lessons.text: If a matching tactic relies on unusual constant storage or volatile loading, document the tactic locally and route it for later cleanup review.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: zero_init @todo and volatile f32 load

naming_conventions.text: No broad naming convention is established by this PR. The only new semantic data name is zero_init, tied to .sdata2:0x804DC6B8 and a local static constant in itdrop.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itdrop.c static const f32 zero_init[1]

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a one-element static const f32 zero_init array and replaced several 0.0f expressions with zero_init[0].
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This resembles named constant/data anchoring; standards say literal/data ownership needs evidence and should not be promoted as normal style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: In it_8026F3D4, loaded zero with zero = *(volatile f32*) &zero_init[0] before chained Vec3 zero assignment.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Volatile local/codegen steering needs explicit verification evidence; bot report supports the function match but not reviewer intent.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Changed manual stack padding sizes in it_8026F3D4 and it_8026F6BC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: PAD_STACK and stack-padding changes are narrow matching tactics and should be revisited when underlying layout or source shape is understood.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 72.66% (+0.02%, +628 bytes), two new matches for it_8026F3D4 and it_8026F6BC, and one broken .sdata2 match from 100.00% to 85.71%.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Approved review with no body text in the loaded slice.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: review by ribbanya state APPROVED

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/it/itdrop.c zero_init or removing the @todo hack.
Search for zero_init in item files and symbols.txt to see whether this remained isolated or became a repeated tactic.
Search for later changes to .sdata2 symbols at 0x804DC6B8, @217, and @218.
Find PRs that fixed or discussed the itdrop .sdata2 regression after PR 2665.
Search for PAD_STACK changes in itdrop.c after this PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: itdrop linked as Matching with visible .sdata2 tradeoff

The bot report credited new matches for it_8026F3D4 and it_8026F6BC, while also reporting a broken .sdata2 match. Treat this as a case where text-section progress and data-section regression were both visible in the PR evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Document volatile/static literal matching hacks as exceptions, not style
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2665 used a documented zero_init hack and volatile f32 load to match it_8026F3D4 while the bot report also showed a .sdata2 regression. This may be a useful example for standards about requiring narrow evidence and cleanup notes for constant-storage and volatile matching tactics.
source_update_candidates.reason: Do not promote the tactic as accepted style; use only as a cautionary example if the curator wants a standards note.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that zero_init is a generally preferred replacement for 0.0f; loaded standards caution against promoting literals into named storage without ownership evidence.
Do not infer reviewer intent from the approval beyond the fact that an approval exists with no body text.
Do not claim full data matching for itdrop; the bot report explicitly lists a broken .sdata2 match.
Do not claim current repository status from this historical PR alone; verify current source before adding path facts.
